text:
Once upon a timeless quiet evening, Charlie rode his bike slowly along the gravel-bordered park path. It was serene, just him, the stars twinkling above, and the gentle rustling of leaves below. He thought about everything that had happened lately.

He remembered the night his brother had moved away. He remembered her sudden death. But most of all, Charlie recalled his first bike ride. It marked a turning point—he learned independence from his dad.

As Charlie drifted into nostalgia, an old pine tree caught his eye. Its trunk cracked like old bones. Beneath the bark, a spider web glimmered proudly. Charlie chuckled and leaned closer. She spun her web effortlessly. It shimmered with tiny stars.

Then Charlie heard a rustle nearby. Panicked, he skidded to a halt. A fluffy fox glanced up with curious eyes before trotting away into the brush.

That night Charlie dreamed of chasing ghosts. They chased him through twisted roads, but he always slipped back through his
